name: Right Ulna
description: Medial bone of the forearm. Its trochlear notch articulates with the trochlea of the humerus, forming the hinge of the elbow joint.
articulations: Trochlea of the humerus (at the trochlear notch) — elbow (hinge) joint
Head of the radius (at the radial notch) — proximal radioulnar (pivot) joint
Ulnar notch of the distal radius — distal radioulnar joint
muscleAttachments: Flexor carpi ulnaris — olecranon and posterior border
clinical: Monteggia fracture: a fracture of the proximal ulna with dislocation of the radial head.
A direct blow to the forearm raised in defence can fracture the shaft (a 'nightstick' fracture).
